sections.paragraph:
φόβητρον and φόβηθρον (φοβέω; different suffixes; s. B-D-F §35, 3; Mlt-H. 110; Thackeray 104), ου, τό (Hippocr., Morb. Sacr. 1 vol. Vl p. 362 L.; Ps.-Pla., Axioch. 367a; Lucian, Alex. 25; Anth. Pal. 11, 189, 3 Düb.; Is 19:17) someth. unusual that causes fear, terrible sight/event, horror. Pl. φόβητρά τε καὶ σημεῖα ἔσται there will be dreadful portents and signs Lk 21:11.—DELG s.v. φέβομαι II.

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

Thackeray Thackeray = HT., A Grammar of the OT in Greek according to the Septuagint—List 6

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

vol. vol. = volume

  1. L. = KLachmann, editor of the Greek NT (1842–50)—List 1, beg.

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1062
